mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment rate exceeding 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and real GDP (GDPC1) signal weakening supply-side momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 70% of cases (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains (PAYEMS) indicate robust labor market demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through increased consumer spending.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with PAYEMS revisions (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation above 2% (CPIAUCSL) forces central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2% for 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint rise in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 65% of cases (Rank IC < 0.04).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units signals weak demand for construction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when HOUST falls below 1,200 thousand units.
